# youtube-legend-cli (full)

> Non-interactive Rust CLI for downloading YouTube subtitles through third-party providers via a native Unix stdin/stdout interface. Single static binary, no daemon, no telemetry.

## Architecture
- clap-derived argument parser (Cli) with FormatArg and LanguageArg enums and seven global flags (--config, --log-level, --log-format, --color, --no-progress, --dry-run, --yes)
- top-level dispatch in commands::run selects batch vs single-URL based on Cli::batch
- ProviderChain walks ProviderA then ProviderB in order, throttled to one request per second
- each Provider implements async fetch_subtitle returning SubtitleInfo and fetch_content returning Vec<u8>
- retry::retry_with_backoff wraps fetch_subtitle with three attempts at 1s, 2s, 4s; honours Retry-After (delta-seconds or RFC 2822 date) with 60s fallback capped at 300s
- provider::robots checks the upstream robots.txt before each request; the Disallow path produces EX_UNAVAILABLE
- cache::cache_path resolves to ~/.cache/youtube-legend-cli/<author>/subtitles/<video_id>/<lang>/<format>.bin
- cache TTL defaults to 24 hours and is overridable via --cache-ttl
- crypto::encrypt_token produces a base64(hex(salt(32) || iv(16) || ciphertext)) string with PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA1 at 100 iterations
- mimalloc is the global allocator in src/main.rs

## Public API surface
- Cli — clap parser; Cli::validate() returns Result<(), String> for flag sanity checks
- AppError — thiserror enum with variants InvalidInput, InvalidUsage, InvalidUrl, NoSubtitle(NoSubtitleReason), ProviderUnavailable, RateLimited, StdinEmpty, Http, Io, Serde, Crypto, SubtitleTooLarge, Internal; #[non_exhaustive]
- AppResult<T> = Result<T, AppError>
- NoSubtitleReason — enum with NotPublished, NotAvailable, AutoGenerated, PaidOrMembers
- run(cli: Cli) -> AppResult<ExitCode> — top-level entry point
- extract::run and batch::run — command dispatch
- convert_format, output_success, output_error — JSON envelope helpers
- cache_path, default_ttl, read_cache, write_cache, invalidate_cache — cache API
- retry_with_backoff, CircuitBreaker — retry helpers
- extract_video_id, srt_to_text — parse helpers
- is_stdin_tty, is_stdout_tty, read_url_from_stdin, read_urls_from_stdin, write_subtitle_to_stdout, write_to_stderr — IO helpers
- normalize_nfc — Unicode NFC (text is pub(crate), reachable only via the crate)
- encrypt_token — crypto
- init_tracing(cli_log_level, cli_log_format, cli_color, quiet) — logging setup; honours YT_LOG_LEVEL and YT_LOG_FORMAT env vars above CLI args
- Provider trait with name(), fetch_subtitle, fetch_content
- Format::Srt, Format::Txt with as_str() and extension()
- SubtitleInfo with video_id, language, format, source_url, byte_size
- ProviderChain::new, with_min_interval, throttle, fetch_subtitle
- ProviderA, ProviderB — concrete provider implementations
- ProviderHeadless (feature = "headless") — optional Chromium/Chrome fallback

## Flags
- --lang: en, pt, es, fr, de, it, plus BCP 47 forms (pt-BR, pt_BR.UTF-8, EN-us). Default en. Malformed/unsupported → exit 2.
- --format: txt or srt. Default txt.
- --timeout: HTTP timeout in seconds. Default 30.
- --verbose: emit tracing events to stderr
- --quiet: suppress all non-error stderr
- --json: emit JSON envelope to stdout
- --batch: read multiple URLs from stdin
- --user-agent: override the default User-Agent (defaults to crate name)
- --cache-ttl: cache TTL in hours (default 24)
- --no-cache: skip cache reads
- --config <PATH>: path to a TOML file overriding the defaults below; CLI flags win on conflict
- --log-level <LEVEL>: error | warn | info | debug | trace. Default warn. Honoured via YT_LOG_LEVEL env var above the CLI value.
- --log-format <FORMAT>: text | json. Default text. Honoured via YT_LOG_FORMAT env var above the CLI value.
- --color <WHEN>: auto | always | never. Default auto. Auto uses isatty on stdout and respects NO_COLOR.
- --no-progress: suppress any progress bars on stderr
- --dry-run: skip network I/O; serve reads from cache only
- --yes: assume yes for any confirmation prompt

## Exit codes (BSD sysexits.h)
- 0 success
- 64 invalid usage or input (EX_USAGE)
- 65 invalid URL (EX_DATAERR)
- 66 no subtitle for the video (EX_NOINPUT)
- 69 all providers unavailable, or rate limited, or robots.txt disallow (EX_UNAVAILABLE)
- 70 internal / IO / HTTP / timeout / crypto / serde error (EX_SOFTWARE)
- 78 configuration error (EX_CONFIG)
- 130 received SIGINT or SIGTERM

On HTTP 429 the CLI honours the Retry-After header in both delta-seconds and RFC 2822 HTTP-date form (60s fallback when absent, capped at 300s) before retrying.

## Cancellation
- SIGINT and SIGTERM are wired through tokio_util::CancellationToken in main.rs
- the first signal triggers cooperative cancellation
- the second signal forces immediate process exit (std::process::exit(130))
- in-flight requests are aborted at the next await point
- process exits with code 130
- the async API exposed by this crate is cancellation-safe at every public await point

## Observability
- tracing-subscriber with EnvFilter is initialised from --log-level (CLI) > YT_LOG_LEVEL (env) > RUST_LOG (env) > default "error"
- 14 public API entry points carry #[tracing::instrument] with explicit field names
- every retry attempt emits event = "retry" with attempt number and next delay
- JSON output is enabled by --log-format json or by YT_LOG_FORMAT=json
- RUSTDOCFLAGS="-D warnings" is enforced in CI for every doc build

## Build targets
- x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu (glibc dynamic)
- x86_64-unknown-linux-musl (fully static)
- aarch64-unknown-linux-musl (ARM64 static)
- x86_64-pc-windows-msvc (Windows 64-bit)
- x86_64-apple-darwin (cross-compile, requires osxcross; continue-on-error in CI)
- aarch64-apple-darwin (cross-compile, requires osxcross; continue-on-error in CI)

## Companion binaries
- youtube-legend-cli (default) — the subtitle fetcher
- snapshot — probes both providers and writes redacted HTML snapshots under tests/fixtures/snapshots/<date>/; consumes the gitignored src/secret_endpoints.rs via #[path = ...] so the upstream hostnames never enter the published rustdoc

## CI
- 12 jobs in ci.yml: test (stable + beta), cross-compile (6 targets), publish-dry-run, msrv, deny, audit, public-api (with sigilo gate), semver-checks, cargo-install, matrix-os (3 OSes), nightly, docs-link-check
- RUSTFLAGS and RUSTDOCFLAGS both set to -D warnings
- lychee offline link check
- 6 cross-compile targets
- msrv job pinned to 1.88.0 (Cargo.toml rust-version); the local rust-toolchain.toml stays on the latest stable for reproducible development
- sigilo gate in public-api job fails the build if pub mod secret_endpoints ever regresses in src/lib.rs

## Security
- AES-256-CBC plus PBKDF2 for token signing
- 50 MiB in-memory cap on decoded subtitle size
- no telemetry, no analytics, no network call home
- cookie store with reqwest rustls
- input validation: URL parsing, video-id 11-char regex, cache path sanitisation
- robots.txt compliance for both providers
- SIGIL: secret_endpoints is pub(crate); upstream hostnames are never visible in rustdoc or public-api baseline
- SECURITY.md: supported versions table, threat model, private reporting path

## Performance baseline
- cache_key_compose: ~34 ns/iter
- url_length_check: 0 ns/iter (sub-ns, rounded down)
- locale_parse_primary_subtag: ~6 ns/iter
- measured on the maintainer's x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu host (2026-06-14, release profile, 1000 samples)
- run with cargo bench --bench cache_bench; CI compiles the bench target via cargo bench --no-run

## Optional
\- Repository: https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/youtube-legend-cli
- Documentation: https://docs.rs/youtube-legend-cli
- crates.io: https://crates.io/crates/youtube-legend-cli
- License: dual MIT OR Apache-2.0
- Authors: Danilo
- Keywords: youtube, subtitles, cli, scraping, srt
\- Categories: command-line-utilities (the web-programming::scraping slug was deprecated on crates.io, see v0.2.7)
- MSRV: 1.88.0 (declared in Cargo.toml rust-version; the local rust-toolchain.toml is the development toolchain, not the MSRV contract)
